texto: There they are. Dancing in a circle. Shadows swinging over the snow. Calling upon the ancestors. Each wearing the sacred shirt. Side stepping to the left in time to the echoing heartbeat of the drums and the echoing yearning of the songs. There they are. Dancing in a circle. Shadows swinging over the firelit snow. Calling upon the ancestors. Expecting something wonderful to happen. Everyone singing. We will live again. We will live again.

texto: Today she feels she is the master of her craft. Today she is free of the grinding tyranny of doubt. The voice that mocks her ambition. The voice that bites and slanders and causes her more heartache than any other voice. Today she is focused, she is exultant. Her every brushstroke like a wake of radiance. Today she can move the paint around the canvas at will. If only painting were like this every day. Without the sudden extinguishing of light, the collapsing of belief, the cursing and flailing, the knots and clenched fists in a world gone suddenly dark.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Glenn Haybittle: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Today she feels she is the master of her craft. texto: Esme skips on ahead. Jumping from one foot to the other, as if she can see markings on the ground he can’t. She is constantly jumping and skipping and twirling with the lightness of falling snow, looking up at him bright with questions, tugging on his hand, dashing off with all the speed her body is capable of and then skipping on the spot up ahead as if consecrating it for his arrival. It is so easy to make her happy that it seems like cheating at times.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Glenn Haybittle: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Esme skips on ahead. Jumping from one foot to the other, as if she can see markings on the ground…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, parenthood, ahead pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
